title: New York imposes one-year moratorium on data centers over 50 megawatts, first in the US
summary: The state of New York approves a pause in the construction of large data centers, eliminating tax exemptions and putting the brakes on the expansion of the sector.

title: DeepMind CEO and progressive leader call for regulation and serious messaging on artificial intelligence
summary: Both the Google DeepMind executive and a US congressman urge setting standards and preventing AI money from silencing public debate.

title: Elon Musk's Colossus 2 data center installed 59 gas turbines without permission, polluting Black communities in Mississippi
summary: The report reveals that the installation released thousands of tons of pollutants, affecting areas with high rates of lung disease.

title: DeepSeek seeks to raise $1.5 billion and prepares for IPO
summary: The Chinese AI startup seeks new capital just weeks after closing a $7 billion round.

title: Video generation startup PixVerse raises $439 million and reaches valuation over $2 billion
summary: The investment reflects continued confidence in the AI video generation market.

title: Oracle suffers 50% stock drop since June and is downgraded by S&P to just one notch above junk bond
summary: The drastic drop has caused Larry Ellison to fall from 2nd to 8th on the billionaires list.

title: Apple introduces iOS 27 with AI-powered Siri features and accelerates development of M7 chips for AI
summary: Siri becomes an integral part of Apple's tools while the company accelerates its own AI chips.

title: Telegram's t.me domain was erroneously suspended for a day and later restored
summary: The suspension affected all Telegram links worldwide, causing service disruptions.

title: Major publishers sue Google for using their content to train artificial intelligence
summary: The lawsuit adds to growing legal battles over the use of copyrighted data in training AI models.

title: Iran exploited mobile network vulnerabilities to locate US military personnel in the Middle East
summary: A report reveals that the Iranian government exploited security weaknesses in telephone networks to track military targets.

title: YouTube and X become gateways to apps that undress images of people
summary: Social platforms are being used as distribution channels for non-consensual sexual deepfake apps.

title: Government Efficiency Department used artificial intelligence for housing policies without revealing how
summary: The federal government used AI models in housing decision-making, but refuses to provide public details on its operation.

title: SoftBank CEO Masayoshi Son says artificial intelligence will need $5 trillion annually by 2040
summary: Son dismisses bubbles and points out that massive AI investment is necessary to sustain technological growth.

title: SpaceX shares hit all-time lows after significant competitor breakthrough
summary: Elon Musk's space company suffers a sharp setback in the stock market after a rival achieved a major technological advance.

title: Nintendo reportedly considering upgrading Switch 2 screen to a 1080p OLED panel
summary: The Japanese company is evaluating improving the visual quality of its next portable console to offer greater sharpness and vivid colors.

title: Intel invests $5.7 billion in its Ireland factory to increase Xeon 6 production
summary: The investment aims to expand manufacturing of next-generation processors based on the Intel 3 process.

title: China doubles chip exports to $177 billion in first half of 2026
summary: The 96% year-over-year increase is attributed to rising memory prices, inflating the value of exports.

title: Nvidia reduces list of authorized customers in Asia to curb smuggling of AI chips
summary: The company sends field inspectors to customers to verify business legitimacy, pressured by Washington.

title: Apple sues OpenAI for alleged hardware secrets theft
summary: The Cupertino company accuses OpenAI of improperly appropriating confidential information about its chips.

title: ChatGPT returns to WhatsApp in Europe after EU order forces Meta to open door to rival bots
summary: The European measure forces Meta to allow competition from AI assistants on its messaging platform.
